dataSourceNote: Based on Slack only — no Granola or Gong data available.

executiveSummaries.size: 2 pilot locations, ~2K calls/week; free pilot → $1.25/resolved call + $0.40/ClearPay at scale
executiveSummaries.status: Signed 4/27. Channel renamed from prosp to partner on 5/1 — deal is live. Kickoff in progress: Carli collecting Athena API credentials, Kennedy (CS/Maya) gathering phone routing and DID requirements. Kapeesh asked Brantley to build a Google Drive SOP to document design partner onboarding learnings.
keyRisks: Resolution rate unproven in ortho — free pilot pricing locks in before we know the numbers
Jim wanted to see a reference customer before signing; we don't have one — delivery must prove the model
Org just 3x'd in size (14→40 physicians) via two acquisitions; implementation complexity may be higher than scoped
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Complete implementation setup: Brantley to share Athena access and Maya DID info with Carli and Kennedy; Kapeesh to build onboarding SOP doc — Owner: Brantley/Kapeesh — Due: This week

executiveSummaries.size: $3,500/month (up to 3K telemed visits) + 1% SolvPay + $0.50/ClearPay; 15–30 physicians, 250 providers total (Texas)
executiveSummaries.champion: Abigail (ops/technical), Dr. Attia (clinical); President is engaged and driving urgency
executiveSummaries.status: Pricing sent and actively being negotiated as of 4/24. Prospect is motivated — framed requests as 'help us make it easier to sign.' Three concession asks: per-encounter or discounted ramp pricing, 6-month first term (not 1-year), and waive $2K impl fee. Separately, opened a design partner conversation on ClearPay for specialty clinics (pVerify couldn't deliver what they need). Ideal start date: June 1.
keyRisks: ECW SolvPay feasibility unconfirmed internally — blocks deal part #1
EMR switch to Epic/Oracle possible in ~1 year — requires contract flexibility
Multiple pricing concessions in flight simultaneously — margin risk
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Teresa/Brantley to resolve ECW SolvPay feasibility and respond to concession requests; Kapeesh to scope specialty ClearPay design partner — Owner: Teresa/Brantley/Kapeesh — Due: ASAP (June 1 is forcing function)

executiveSummaries.size: 3 clinics + 1 standalone urgent ortho opening June 1; MRR TBD
executiveSummaries.champion: Jennifer Hazelton (Dir. Marketing, ex-PhysicianOne), Christopher LaFave (Executive Director)
executiveSummaries.status: Strong 4/8 full-team walkthrough with exec director, ops manager, clinical manager, and marketing director — all four attended, asked operational questions, confirmed urgent ortho workflow fit. Team requested ~1 week for internal review, then a follow-up demo + pricing call. Jennifer is already sold; June 1 opening creates a real forcing function. Christopher pushed for a live ECW data-flow demo.
keyRisks: ECW integration confidence is a hard gate — Christopher wants to see data flowing, not just hear about it
Referral workflow gap may block expansion to non-urgent ortho locations
No confirmed follow-up call as of 4/12 — reconnect may have slipped
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Brantley to confirm follow-up demo + pricing call is scheduled; send ECW integration packet — Owner: Brantley — Due: Overdue (was Mon/Tues week of 4/14), chase ASAP

executiveSummaries.champion: Rikin Shah (Chief of Staff) — not final DM
executiveSummaries.status: As of 4/7, prospect is 6–9 months out from any real timeline. Won't replace their CTO-built scheduler ('too many resources poured in'). Brantley surfaced an alternative — Solv as GMB acquisition layer running in parallel with their scheduler — but internal appetite for that model is unknown. Rikin is openly piloting a competing voice agent and expressed skepticism about Maya.
keyRisks: 6–9 month pushback likely means no deal in 2026
Competing voice agent already in active pilot — Maya entry point closed for now
Solv's appetite for acquisition-only, scheduler-light deal is undefined
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Internal Solv decision on whether acquisition-only deal model is worth pursuing; if yes, schedule tech call with TPC CTO — Owner: Brantley/Kapeesh — Due: TBD

executiveSummaries.status: Paused since 3/11 due to unresolved Assort Health contract exit. Jon proactively communicated the block rather than ghosting and confirmed intent to return to Solv when clear. No timeline given. Last touch was 3/30 (friendly, no asks). Bryan joined the channel on 4/22 — monitoring status only. This deal was near-signature in late February before the Assort issue surfaced.
keyRisks: Zero visibility into Assort exit timeline — could be months
Deal momentum lost after being near-signature; restarting will require re-engagement
Competitive risk during pause window
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Passive monitoring — periodic check-in every 2 weeks; next touch ~mid-May — Owner: Brantley

executiveSummaries.champion: Randy Johansen (board member, former AFC CEO) — intro via Heather at UCA happy hour
executiveSummaries.status: Very early stage. First exploratory call 4/24 after UCA happy hour intro. Randy is cash-constrained context aware — org opened 3 locations at once and stretched itself thin (Kennesaw location has been especially painful). Randy requested ClearPay/Maya materials and planned to meet with the OS1 team to assess if they're in a position to move. Reconnect planned for Friday/Monday week of 4/28.
keyRisks: Cash-constrained org — budget availability is a real question before any sale can move
Randy is a board member, not an operator — unclear internal decision authority
No EHR or operational contacts identified yet
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Brantley to send ClearPay/Maya materials (Evan Cory involved) and confirm reconnect call — Owner: Brantley/Evan — Due: Week of 4/28 (may be slightly overdue)

executiveSummaries.champion: Katia Sarfian (Administrator) — owner Dr. Tovmassian is the blocker
executiveSummaries.status: Dr. Tovmassian rejected ClearPay on 3/16: 'doesn't make sense for our practice, 90% of patients are recurring.' Current point-of-care estimate model doesn't fit a primary care recurring-patient base. Brantley followed up 3/24 requesting a leadership call to explore alternative models — no response. Not pushing hard given they're an existing Mederi urgent care partner.
keyRisks: Owner-level objection is substantive — product-market fit genuinely doesn't exist here yet
Pushing too hard risks damaging the existing Mederi relationship
No clear alternative model to offer that solves the recurring-patient objection
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Low-priority dormant follow-up; revisit when ClearPay evolves a primary care use case — Owner: Brantley — Due: No near-term action

leadershipTable.keyNotes: Signed 4/27; kickoff in progress — Athena API and Maya DID setup underway.
Pricing out, prospect negotiating concessions; ECW SolvPay feasibility is the critical blocker.
Strong 4/8 walkthrough; ECW live-demo required to advance; follow-up call may have slipped.
6–9 month timeline pushback; won't replace homegrown scheduler; competing voice agent in pilot.
Blocked by Assort Health contract exit since 3/11; Jon will re-engage when resolved.
UCA intro; cash-constrained org, reconnect call week of 4/28 pending materials share.
Owner rejected ClearPay outright — primary care recurring-patient model isn't a fit yet.
